name: RABBIT-CAGE-11
title: Dispatch template does not require subagents to call tdd-step.sh — full TDD state machine bypassed
description: The subagent-launch-template.txt injected by dispatch-feature-edit.sh instructs subagents to perform TDD (write test, confirm fail, implement, confirm pass) but never requires or instructs them to call tdd-step.sh. As a result, subagents do ad-hoc testing without advancing the formal TDD state machine. This violates R4 (TDD step transitions go through tdd-step.sh) and workflow-rules Section 2 (full TDD step sequence managed by tdd-step.sh on every feature touch, no partial shortcuts). Observed: the CLAUDE.md one-liner dispatch wrote a test and ran it manually but never called tdd-step.sh; the feature.json tdd_state was never transitioned. The fix must ensure the dispatch template explicitly instructs subagents to call tdd-step.sh at each state boundary (test-red, impl, test-green), or alternatively that the main session orchestrates tdd-step.sh transitions around each dispatch leg.

history.note: Fixed via option B: created rabbit-feature-touch skill that orchestrates tdd-step.sh transitions in main session around each dispatch leg. Registered in feature.json surface.skills. Test test-RABBIT-CAGE-11-feature-touch-skill.sh added and passing.
